citation.description: 'ChatGPT / connector-limited sessions' heading with pointer to docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md

criteria.criterion: A ChatGPT-specific guidance file or clearly labeled section is discoverable from AGENTS.md without bloating the canonical startup context. Verify: grep -qi 'chatgpt' AGENTS.md.

citation.description: Six-dimension capability checklist at lines 12-18: 'Tracker worktree' (line 14) and 'Gerrit availability' (line 18) present; all six dimensions enumerated.

criteria.criterion: Guidance includes an environment capability check covering local checkout, native rebar/rebar-mcp, tracker worktree, network/remote access, GitHub connector, and Gerrit availability. Verify: grep -qi 'tracker worktree' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'gerrit' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md.

citation.description: Section 2 explicitly states 'read-only mirror' and 'GitHub Issues are not a substitute for the Rebar tracker'.

criteria.criterion: Guidance states that the GitHub repository is a read-only mirror for code changes and that GitHub Issues are not a substitute for the Rebar tracker. Verify: grep -qi 'read-only mirror' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md.

citation.description: Summary checklist line 98: 'Used native search-before-create when the live tracker was reachable.' (hyphenated form present).

criteria.criterion: Guidance requires search-before-create and native parent/child creation whenever the live tracker is accessible. Verify: grep -qi 'search-before-create' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md.

citation.description: Section 3 lines 41-44: 'Never hand-author, modify, or delete event files directly — the tracker is an append-only event log'.

criteria.criterion: Guidance prohibits hand-authoring, modifying, or deleting event files and explains the append-only reason. Verify: grep -qi 'append-only' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md.

citation.description: Decision rule at lines 20-27 defines 'impossible' precisely; fallback payload at lines 50-65 references export.schema.json (line 50) and schema_version: 1 (line 52), with all required fields enumerated.

criteria.criterion: Guidance defines precisely when native ticket creation counts as 'impossible' and requires producing a reviewable ticket payload conforming to export.schema.json's required fields plus a transparent capability-gap report. Verify: grep -qi 'export.schema.json' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'schema_version' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md.

citation.description: Section 5 (lines 67-92): names rebar import as the only sanctioned path (line 69), describes dry-run behavior including no counter/no warning (lines 83-86), recommends would_create+skipped comparison (lines 87-89), requires rebar validate or rebar fsck afterward (line 91).

criteria.criterion: The documented exceptional import mechanism names rebar import as the only sanctioned path, correctly describes dry-run behavior (no warning/no counter for missing ticket_id/ticket_type), recommends would_create+skipped==total comparison, and requires rebar validate or rebar fsck afterward. Verify: grep -qi 'rebar import' && grep -qi 'rebar validate|rebar fsck' && grep -qi 'dry-run'.

citation.description: File exists with 5 test functions covering: (1) AGENTS.md link, (2) no GitHub Issues substitution, (3) no Contents API affirmative write, (4) rebar import as only ingest, (5) fallback fields match export.schema.json required array.

criteria.criterion: A lightweight documentation test (tests/unit/test_chatgpt_guide_links.py) asserts all 5 specified invariants and exits 0 only if all hold.
